claim_boundary: TICKET132 closes one RH admissibility defect and proves three exact pointwise-boundary or route countermodel theorems. None of the four conjectures is proved or refuted, and none is currently close to proof. No conjecture proof or counterexample is claimed.

riemann.proved_statement: Let L_+(f)=integral f(x)e^(x/2)dx and L_-(f)=integral f(x)e^(-x/2)dx. The TICKET129 rational-bump core can be projected continuously and effectively onto ker(L_+) intersect ker(L_-). The projected set is countable and dense in the two-moment constrained test-function space.
riemann.proof: For the even standard bump b, put psi_0(x)=b(x) and psi_1(x)=b(x-1). If I=integral b(x)e^(x/2)dx>0, evenness gives the normalized moment matrix [[1,e^(1/2)],[1,e^(-1/2)]]. Its determinant is e^(-1/2)-e^(1/2)<0. Hence P(f)=f-(psi_0,psi_1)A^(-1)(L_+(f),L_-(f)) is a continuous projection onto the common kernel. P is the identity on that kernel, so applying P to a countable dense core remains countable and is dense in the constrained subspace. All moments are computable, making the projected descriptions effective.

route_decision.retain: countable effective core plus strict-negative interval search
route_decision.discard: use unconstrained rational bumps as though they already satisfy the two exact Weil moment conditions

riemann.proof_boundary: This closes the two-moment admissibility defect of the enumerable core. It proves neither Weil positivity on that core nor RH, and it does not replace the need to freeze one exact published normalization.

collatz.proved_statement: In the product space of positive accelerated valuation sequences, the codes generated by positive odd integers form a countable dense set. Under every non-atomic Borel probability on the code space this set has measure zero. Every such natural code is eventually stable in the canonical-residue sense of TICKET131.
collatz.proof: Countability follows because positive odd integers are countable. A basic open set fixes one finite valuation word. Its exact cylinder is one odd residue r modulo a power-of-two modulus M, and every r+kM with k>=0 is a positive natural realizer. Hence every nonempty basic open set contains infinitely many natural codes, proving density. A countable set has measure zero for every non-atomic Borel probability. TICKET131 proves that each fixed natural start has eventually constant canonical residues.

topological_measure_contract.ambient_space: positive-integer valuation sequences with product topology
topological_measure_contract.countability: image of the positive odd integers
topological_measure_contract.density: every finite word cylinder contains r+kM for all k>=0
topological_measure_contract.nullity: countable union of zero-mass singletons under any non-atomic measure
topological_measure_contract.stabilization: every natural code has eventually constant canonical residues

route_decision.retain: exact pointwise natural residue plus ordinary magnitude
route_decision.discard: use product-topology separation or measure-zero code mass alone to exclude positive-integer counterexamples

collatz.proof_boundary: The dense-null theorem explains why finite-prefix classifiers and probability estimates can both look strong and still miss the pointwise Collatz obligation. It proves no orbit descends.

goldbach.proved_statement: For every m>=1, the normalized binary Goldbach singular-series multiplier of N=2^m is exactly one. Therefore no finite small-odd-divisor stratification can remove the uniform minimal-main case: arbitrarily large powers of two remain outside every boosted stratum. Under the fixed TICKET128-131 endpoint constants, K=56 has positive lower-certificate margin at multiplier one, while the same lower-certificate architecture with K=57 does not.
goldbach.proof: The arithmetic multiplier is product over odd p dividing N of (p-1)/(p-2). For N=2^m the index set is empty, so the product is one. Powers of two are unbounded. Thus every proof that only gains margin from a bounded list of odd divisors leaves an infinite minimal-main stratum. Substitution of the fixed rational major floor, log lower bound, and contamination bound gives the displayed exact K=56 and K=57 margins.

route_decision.retain: arithmetic stratification as workload reduction for boosted classes
route_decision.discard: expect finite small-divisor stratification alone to close all even integers

goldbach.proof_boundary: This identifies an unavoidable infinite hard stratum. It does not prove the K=56 pointwise residual estimate or Goldbach for powers of two.

twin_prime.proved_statement: For every finite sieve level z, there is an infinite arithmetic progression of odd n such that neither n nor n+2 has a prime divisor at most z, yet both n and n+2 are composite.
twin_prime.proof: For each prime p<=z choose a residue a_p avoiding 0 and -2; use a_2=1, a_3=2, and a_p=1 for p>=5. CRT gives one locally admissible class modulo P=product(p<=z)p. Choose distinct primes q,r>z and add n=0 mod q and n=-2 mod r. CRT gives one class modulo Pqr. Every sufficiently large member has q as a proper divisor of n and r as a proper divisor of n+2, while retaining the same small-prime-clean local signature.

route_decision.retain: actual unbounded Type II information and parity-sensitive exact-gap functional
route_decision.discard: promote any fixed finite local sieve-clean signature to twin primality

twin_prime.proof_boundary: The CRT construction is a countermodel to finite local certification, not a counterexample to the Twin Prime conjecture. It does not rule out parity-sensitive methods using unbounded analytic information.

proof_viability.advance: the enumerable core now preserves the two exact moment constraints
proof_viability.remaining: prove nonnegativity on every projected core element or find a strict negative interval
proof_viability.proximity: not close, but the test space is now logically correct

proof_viability.advance: natural codes are proved countable, dense, null, and residue-stabilizing
proof_viability.remaining: a pointwise Archimedean descent theorem on the natural subset
proof_viability.proximity: not close; topology and probability are now ruled out as standalone bridges

proof_viability.advance: powers of two are isolated as an unavoidable infinite minimal-main stratum
proof_viability.remaining: a K=56 pointwise residual theorem on powers of two and rough even integers
proof_viability.proximity: conditional architecture is precise, decisive minor-arc estimate open

proof_viability.advance: an exact infinite CRT family defeats every fixed local sieve level
proof_viability.remaining: unbounded Type II information plus a parity-sensitive exact-gap lower bound
proof_viability.proximity: not close; finite local state is now formally excluded

literature_boundary.citation: Connes and Consani, The Scaling Hamiltonian
literature_boundary.role: Source for the two multiplicative moment conditions in a Weil-positivity formulation.

literature_boundary.citation: Suzuki, Weil's quadratic form via the screw function
literature_boundary.role: Current continuous-function/operator-limit framework; no RH proof is imported.

literature_boundary.citation: Kramer, Adaptive Search in Collatz Exponent-Code Space
literature_boundary.role: Current exponent-code diagnostics; explicitly not a Collatz verification method.

literature_boundary.citation: Alsetri and Shao, Density versions of the binary Goldbach problem
literature_boundary.role: Primary evidence that density conclusions remain distinct from all-even pointwise Goldbach.

literature_boundary.citation: Ford and Maynard, On the theory of prime producing sieves
literature_boundary.role: Primary Type I/II lower-bound framework; substantial Type II input is necessary.

None of the four conjectures is proved or refuted, and none is currently close to proof. No conjecture proof or counterexample is claimed.
